gomodel:run-figure2-005/tir1/part_of.snippet: TIR-1 required for intestinal defence response to bacteria

gomodel:run-figure2-005/sek1/part_of.snippet: SEK-1 functions in the MAPK cascade

gomodel:run-figure2-005/daf16/part_of.snippet: TF-endpoint pattern: DAF-16 part_of regulation of DNA-templated transcription; figure shows DAF-16 -> antimicrobial defence genes
gomodel:run-figure2-005/daf16/part_of.justification: DAF-16/FOXO is a DNA-binding transcription factor; its figure role is activating antimicrobial defence gene transcription in the nucleus. GO:0006355 was not among the 25 retrieved annotations, so tagged as inference per the TF-endpoint pattern (transcription is the intervening module to the gene set, which is out of pathway scope).

gomodel:run-figure2-005/skn1/part_of.snippet: SKN-1/Nrf2 mediates the oxidative/cellular stress response

gomodel:run-figure2-005/hsf1/part_of.snippet: HSF-1 drives the heat shock / cellular stress response

gomodel:run-figure2-005/atf7/part_of.snippet: ATF-7 mediates PMK-1-dependent antimicrobial defence gene expression

gomodel:run-figure2-005/hsp60/part_of.snippet: HSP-60 is induced during and required for the mitochondrial UPR

gomodel:run-figure2-005/atfs1/part_of.snippet: ATFS-1 is the master transcriptional regulator of the mitochondrial UPR

gomodel:run-figure2-005/hif1/part_of.snippet: HIF-1 mediates the hypoxia response and contributes to intestinal defence

gomodel:run-figure2-005/zip4/part_of.snippet: ZIP-4 contributes to defence against Gram-negative bacteria upstream of ZIP-2

gomodel:run-figure2-005/cebp1/part_of.snippet: CEBP-1 positively regulates transcription; in the figure it activates ZIP-2
gomodel:run-figure2-005/cebp1/part_of.term_label: positive regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II

gomodel:run-figure2-005/zip2/part_of.snippet: ZIP-2 drives the early antimicrobial/ESRE defence response to P. aeruginosa

gomodel:run-figure2-005/fshr1/part_of.snippet: FSHR-1 GPCR signaling contributes to intestinal defence against Gram-negative bacteria

gomodel:run-figure2-005/nhr86/part_of.snippet: NHR-86/HNF-4 regulates transcription; figure shows it activating antimicrobial defence genes

gomodel:run-figure2-005/atfs1/occurs_in.snippet: ATFS-1 accumulates in the nucleus to drive UPRmt transcription

gomodel:run-figure2-005/daf16/occurs_in.snippet: DAF-16 acts in the nucleus (DNA-binding TF)
gomodel:run-figure2-005/daf16/occurs_in.justification: DAF-16/FOXO is a DNA-binding transcription factor that must act in the nucleus to drive defence-gene transcription; no nucleus CC was among the 25 retrieved annotations, so tagged as inference.

gomodel:run-figure2-005/skn1/occurs_in.snippet: SKN-1 acts in the nucleus (DNA-binding TF)
gomodel:run-figure2-005/skn1/occurs_in.justification: SKN-1/Nrf2 is a DNA-binding transcription factor that translocates to the nucleus to drive stress-response transcription; no nucleus CC was among the 25 retrieved annotations, so tagged as inference.

gomodel:run-figure2-005/hsf1/occurs_in.snippet: HSF-1 acts in the nucleus (DNA-binding TF)
gomodel:run-figure2-005/hsf1/occurs_in.justification: HSF-1 is a DNA-binding transcription factor acting in the nucleus to drive heat-shock/stress-response transcription; no nucleus CC was among the 25 retrieved annotations, so tagged as inference.

gomodel:run-figure2-005/cebp1/occurs_in.snippet: CEBP-1 acts in the nucleus (DNA-binding TF)
gomodel:run-figure2-005/cebp1/occurs_in.justification: CEBP-1 is a bZIP DNA-binding transcription factor acting in the nucleus; no nucleus CC was among the 25 retrieved annotations, so tagged as inference.

gomodel:run-figure2-005/tir1/causal/gomo.snippet: TIR-1/SARM adaptor acts upstream of and directly positively regulates the NSY-1 MAPKKK in the C. elegans signaling module (figure: Pyocyanin->TIR-1->NSY-1; here TIR-1 product is directly upstream, positive, of NSY-1)

gomodel:run-figure2-005/nsy1/causal/gomo.snippet: NSY-1 MAPKKK phosphorylates and directly positively regulates the SEK-1 MAPKK (figure: NSY-1->SEK-1 pointed arrow in the p38 cascade)

gomodel:run-figure2-005/sek1/causal/gomo.snippet: SEK-1 MAPKK phosphorylates and directly positively regulates the PMK-1 p38 MAPK (figure: SEK-1->PMK-1 pointed arrow in the p38 cascade)

gomodel:run-figure2-005/pmk1/causal/gomo.snippet: PMK-1/p38 directly phosphorylates the bZIP transcription factor ATF-7, converting it to a transcriptional activator of defence genes (figure: PMK-1->ATF-7 pointed arrow); PMK-1 product directly positively regulates ATF-7

Figure draws a line linking the PMK-1/p38 module to the DAF-16/FOXO stack (PMK-1 -> DAF-16, positive), explicitly marked as uncertain (confidence 0.3). Modeled as causally upstream, positive effect, mechanism/directness unverified.

gomodel:run-figure2-005/aak2/causal/gomo.snippet: Figure draws AMPK -> HIF-1 pointed arrow in the central module, explicitly flagged as an uncertain linkage (confidence 0.45). Modeled as AMPK causally upstream, positive effect, of HIF-1; mechanism unverified.

gomodel:run-figure2-005/zip4/causal/gomo.snippet: Figure draws ZIP-4 -> ZIP-2 pointed arrow (right bZIP module), flagged uncertain (confidence 0.45). ZIP-4 is a bZIP TF; it indirectly positively regulates ZIP-2 (transcription/expression is the intervening module). Mechanism unverified.

gomodel:run-figure2-005/cebp1/causal/gom.snippet: Figure draws CEBP-1/2 -> ZIP-2 pointed arrow (right bZIP module, confidence 0.9). CEBP-1 is a bZIP TF; it indirectly positively regulates ZIP-2 (gene expression is the intervening module). No PMID for this specific step was retrievable.

gomodel:run-figure2-005/atfs1/causal/gom.snippet: ATFS-1 is the UPRmt master transcription factor that induces mitochondrial chaperone genes including hsp-60; it indirectly positively regulates HSP-60 (transcription is the intervening module). Models the figure's 'Mitochondria/UPRmt -> HSP-60' arrow via the UPRmt TF.

gomodel:run-figure2-005/tir1/has_small_m.snippet: Figure: Pyocyanin -> TIR-1/SARM1 pointed activation arrow (confidence 0.9). Pyocyanin (P. aeruginosa phenazine toxin; tentative ChEBI:27592 — curator to verify) modeled as a small-molecule activator of TIR-1 activity (acts via LRO stress/ROS).

gomodel:run-figure2-005/nsy1/has_small_m.snippet: Figure: Pyocyanin -> NSY-1/MAPKKK pointed arrow, low confidence (0.3, unconfirmed in verification). Pyocyanin (tentative ChEBI:27592 — curator to verify) modeled as small-molecule activator of NSY-1.

gomodel:run-figure2-005/aak2/has_small_m.snippet: Figure: mtROS -> AMPK pointed arrow (confidence 0.8). Mitochondrial reactive oxygen species (ChEBI:26523) modeled as a small-molecule activator of AAK-2/AMPK kinase activity.

gomodel:run-figure2-005/hsf1/has_small_m.snippet: Figure: HSF-1 <-> ROS connection, direction uncertain (confidence 0.3). Reactive oxygen species (ChEBI:26523) modeled as a small-molecule activator of HSF-1; directionality flagged uncertain for curator review.

gomodel:run-figure2-005/hif1/has_small_m.snippet: Figure: +Fe3+ -> HIF-1 arrow, flagged uncertain (confidence 0.35). Iron(3+) (ChEBI:29034) modeled as a small-molecule regulator (activator as drawn) of HIF-1; note iron classically promotes HIF degradation, so sign is uncertain — for curator review.
